title: Align EDHOC wire format with RFC 9528
description: Rust EDHOC self-roundtrips but is not RFC 9528 interoperable: Message 1 emits obsolete METHOD_CORR and does not enforce CDDL; Message 2 places C_R outside CIPHERTEXT_2 and has wrong CBOR structure; Message 3 is raw ciphertext rather than bstr; connection identifiers omit canonical negative integers; suite rejection lacks error-code-2/SUITES_R negotiation. Rewrite messages/transcript inputs against RFC 9528/9529 vectors.
design: Use RFC 9529 published vectors as the independent oracle. Do not use Rust initiator/responder self-roundtrip as proof of interoperability.
acceptance_criteria: RFC 9529 message_1/message_2/message_3 and transcript/key vectors match byte-for-byte; METHOD=0 SIGN/SIGN; C_R is authenticated inside PLAINTEXT_2; message 2/3 CDDL and connection identifiers are canonical; malformed/trailing EAD rejects or is explicitly supported; error-code-2 negotiation works; host and Cortex-M checks, three independent reviews pass.
notes: Implemented on integration/oscore-recovery after bc657b335. Rust EDHOC now follows RFC 9528 message_1/2/3 CDDL, METHOD=0, authenticated C_R, canonical bstr envelopes/connection IDs, integer KDF labels, PRK_out/PRK_exporter chain, correct OSCORE ID mapping, strict X25519/Ed25519 checks, bounded multi-form credentials, two-phase ID_CRED lookup, suite error handling, EAD/trailing rejection, and RFC 9528 ID_CRED unsorted-map exception. Independent RFC 9529 literals cover messages, TH_2/3/4, PRKs, Master Secret/Salt, and endpoint IDs. Final gates: 69 host tests including vectors/doctest, no_std tests, thumbv7em-none-eabi/eabihf checks, strict library Clippy, diff check; repeated review cycles converged with no unresolved in-scope correctness findings. No AWS required.
close_reason: RFC 9528 wire format and RFC 9529 independent vectors implemented; host/no_std/Cortex-M/Clippy gates and repeated reviews pass.
